title: woof agent role configuration
description: Project-side declaration at .woof/agents.toml. Defines which dispatch wrapper (cld/cod), which model, and which extra flags are used per role. Woof builds the full subprocess invocation dynamically from these entries; no hard-coded model IDs or shell aliases anywhere in the tool. If absent, MVP defaults apply (in-session planner, cld -p story-executor with default model, cod critiquer with default model, all with empty MCP set).

roles.description: Roles invoked by woof. Any subset may be defined; missing roles fall back to MVP defaults. orchestrator and gate-resolver are constrained to in-session execution and don't accept harness/model overrides — entries here are advisory metadata for them.

default_minutes.description: Per-subprocess timeout. Driver wraps cld/cod invocations with `timeout`. Hitting the timeout writes gate.md with triggered_by=['timeout'].

harness.description: Dispatch wrapper. cld for Claude subprocesses, cod for Codex subprocesses, in-session for roles that must share the user's interactive session (orchestrator, gate-resolver).

model.description: Model identifier passed to the underlying CLI via the wrapper's '--' separator.

think.description: cld only. Toggles extended thinking via the wrapper's -t flag.

mcp.description: MCP server names. Empty array (default) = no MCPs (token-saving). Wrappers default to no MCPs even when this key is absent.

flags.description: Arbitrary additional pass-through args appended after the wrapper's own options. Useful for effort flags, model-specific params, etc.
